description:
You are acting as a QA-automation engineer who has just pulled a fresh copy of a project that already contains a CSV file describing all manual test scenarios.  
Your goal is to extract and prepare a quick “action report” for all still-open **critical** scenarios so that your teammates can immediately see what is left to do.

Existing project layout (already present when the session starts):
• /home/user/projects/qa/test_scenarios/scenarios.csv   ← the raw input file  
• /home/user/projects/qa/reports/                        ← empty directory you will place your output in  

scenarios.csv format (comma-separated, one header line followed by one data line per scenario):
id,title,severity,status,owner

Task you must accomplish:
1. Create a new file /home/user/projects/qa/reports/critical_summary.log.  
2. Read /home/user/projects/qa/test_scenarios/scenarios.csv and select ONLY those lines that meet **both** conditions:  
   a) severity == critical (case-insensitive)  
   b) status  != closed  (case-insensitive)  
3. Sort the resulting lines by the numeric id field (ascending).  
4. Transform each selected line into a single JSON object having EXACTLY the three keys (in this order): id, title, owner.  
   • id must remain a string, title and owner must preserve original capitalization.  
5. Write each JSON object on its own line (no surrounding array, no pretty printing) into critical_summary.log.  
   Example of the required line format:  
   {"id":"104","title":"System reboot on malformed packet","owner":"emma"}  
6. After writing all JSON lines, append ONE final summary line that looks like below (no JSON, no leading/trailing spaces):  
   ### TOTAL_OPEN_CRITICAL=<N>  
   Replace <N> with the count of lines you wrote *before* this summary line.  
7. Ensure the file ends with a single trailing newline character.  
8. Do **not** modify scenarios.csv; create any temporary files you need inside /home/user/projects/qa/work/.  
   (Create that directory if it does not exist.)

Important verification details:
• critical_summary.log must be located exactly at /home/user/projects/qa/reports/critical_summary.log.  
• The number of JSON lines and the value in TOTAL_OPEN_CRITICAL must match.  
• Field order in each JSON object must be id, title, owner.  
• The summary line must appear exactly once and be the last line.  
• The file must be world-readable (chmod 644 or more permissive).

You will likely need no more than 5–10 shell commands to finish the task. Use any common POSIX tools (cut, awk, grep, sed, sort, etc.) you prefer. You are free to echo, cat, and inspect intermediate output as needed.

When you believe you are done, you can display the final file to verify its correctness.
truth:
Input file contents that already exist when the session begins:
/home/user/projects/qa/test_scenarios/scenarios.csv
(id,title,severity,status,owner)
1,"Login with invalid password",low,closed,alice
2,"Login with valid password",medium,closed,bob
15,"Password reset email not sent",high,open,carol
23,"Profile picture upload fails",medium,open,alice
42,"Database deadlock during signup",critical,open,dave
55,"Race condition on logout",critical,closed,emma
71,"Session not terminated on password change",high,open,bob
85,"Crash on importing malformed CSV",critical,reopened,carol
104,"System reboot on malformed packet",critical,open,emma
175,"Two-factor code not accepted",high,open,dave
200,"Memory leak in report generator",critical,in-progress,frank

Expected output file after the agent’s work:
/home/user/projects/qa/reports/critical_summary.log  (chmod 644)

{"id":"42","title":"Database deadlock during signup","owner":"dave"}
{"id":"85","title":"Crash on importing malformed CSV","owner":"carol"}
{"id":"104","title":"System reboot on malformed packet","owner":"emma"}
{"id":"200","title":"Memory leak in report generator","owner":"frank"}
### TOTAL_OPEN_CRITICAL=4
⏎  (single trailing newline)

Additional hidden checks:
• File must contain exactly 5 lines (4 JSON + 1 summary).  
• JSON objects must appear in numerical id order: 42 < 85 < 104 < 200.  
• The summary line value must equal “4”.  
• No extra blank lines or spaces allowed after the trailing newline.  
• Directory /home/user/projects/qa/work/ may exist and may contain temporary files; it is ignored during grading.
name: task_000000_d401a6c7
